qa.user_verbatim_complaint: 加分太严重了，应该是最好的分数是+5，最差的分数是-40。agent是否知道自己的分数？此外用户打分现在每次只展示543，不展示更低的，这是严重问题。所以不要用ask user question模式，而是用happy原生的option模式，在close最后再问用户，而不是在运行close的时候。最后就是为了让用户有充分的参考，应该在close的时候自我总结这个session做了什么没做什么，满足了用户什么需求，没有满足什么，出现了哪些bug，出现了哪些改进空间。然后修复changelog-analyst bulk-commit bug (BULK=false时仍执行bulk行为) 和跨session commit干扰无隔离机制。

Test now fails when collected. Not in this cycle's manifest scope (per-task-scoped per qa.md Phase 5), but represents a cross-cycle regression-risk surface for any global pytest run.

All three iter-1 in-file/process defects RESOLVED in iter-2: test-writer artifacts produced (10 tests, 10/10 pytest pass), close.md stdout-vs-file dual-contract distinction at lines 407-409, changelog-analyst.md Phase 0 defers to Phase 2 at line 76-77.

qa.overall_assessment: PASS (iter-2 verdict flip from iter-1 FAIL). All 6 original acceptance criteria re-verified (EVENT_DELTAS max=+5/min=-40/n=39; AskUserQuestion absent and <options> with all 6 values present; session summary section present; BULK=false whitelist + ABORT guard present; foreign_session_candidate classification present). All 3 iter-1 defects RESOLVED: (FIND-1) test-writer artifacts created — tests/generated/20260524-172805/ contains 10 test_AC_*.py + manifest.json (10 active_tests) + test-writer-report on disk; pytest 10/10 pass in 0.03s; no TEST_INCOMPLETE sentinels. (FIND-2) commands/close.md:407-409 add explicit 'Two distinct final-line contracts — DO NOT confuse' block naming both channels and stating 'two different output channels'. (FIND-3) agents/changelog-analyst.md Phase 0 lines 75-77 now 'deferring staging decision to Phase 2' — warn-only/classification; old 'staging anyway' / 'Do NOT exclude' tokens absent. Codex adversarial review (gpt-5.5 xhigh) confirmed PASS justified with zero blocking findings.

e2e_enforcement.skip_carve_out_rationale: Non-UI cycle: 3 modified files are a bash CLI script (score-update.sh), an orchestrator command prose file (commands/close.md), and an agent prompt file (agents/changelog-analyst.md). No rendered UI, no browser interaction, no Playwright applicable. Per context setup.applicability='N/A' and reason='non-UI -- CLI script + agent-prompt edit'.

manifest_verification._doc: STANDARD tier + test_writer_expected=true in context, but per-task active manifest tests/generated/20260524-172805/manifest.json is ABSENT and global index has no entry for this task. This is a Phase 5 item 5 critical finding.

blast_radius_phase2._doc: Skipped — blast-radius-tool.py invocation not part of this cycle (BA did not produce a Phase 1 map; blast_radius_map_path is null in context).

all_findings.issue: FIND-1: Phase 5 test-writer artifacts missing. context-20260524-172805.json sets complexity_tier=STANDARD, risk_level=medium, test_writer_expected=true. Per agents/qa.md Phase 5 item 5: 'if test_writer_expected = true AND per-task manifest is missing... emit a critical finding'. The orchestrator's dispatch prompt instructed QA to 'note but not fail' on this, but that instruction conflicts with qa.md's hard contract — per Counter-Evidence Authority 'Exception — contract violations', QA records the finding at full critical severity.
all_findings.recommendation: Run test-writer subagent for task 20260524-172805 to produce manifest + report; rerun QA Phase 5. Alternative: if these are truly deterministic config/doc edits with no test-writable behavior, BA should re-issue context with _test_writer_skip_reason set, and Dev should re-run; after-the-fact skip is a process patch and should not be normalized.

all_findings.issue: FIND-2: stdout output contract contradiction. Line 405 says '/close echoes CLOSE: ... as the last line of /close [stdout]'. Line 432 says '<options> at the VERY END of the orchestrator's text message to the user'. Both cannot be the last/very-end of the same stdout stream. This threatens AC-03's clause (d) post-option contract because the runtime parser or downstream consumers may receive ambiguous final-line state. Codex adversarial review confirmed this contradiction.
all_findings.recommendation: Reconcile by clarifying that the CLOSE: final-line contract applies to the close-report FILE (per line 391 'the LAST non-empty line of the file MUST be EXACTLY one of the legal CLOSE: forms') and NOT to /close's stdout. /close's stdout final output should be the <options> block. Explicitly add a 'Note: stdout final output != close-report file final line' clarification at line 405.

all_findings.issue: FIND-3: Phase 0 (dispatch-snapshot check) vs Phase 2 (foreign_session_candidate) instruction conflict. Lines 75-77 say 'staging anyway / Do NOT exclude' for files appearing after dispatch. Lines 102-106 say non-whitelisted files are 'classified as foreign_session_candidate and excluded from staging'. A file that appears after dispatch AND is not in the whitelist receives contradictory directives. This could reintroduce cross-session staging if Phase 0 wins. Codex adversarial review flagged this as a real instruction conflict.
all_findings.recommendation: Update lines 75-77 to clarify Phase 0 is warn-only at dispatch-snapshot time AND that the final staging decision is made in Phase 2 (where the whitelist filter applies and the foreign_session_candidate exclusion takes effect). Explicitly state that 'staging anyway' applies only to BULK=true, OR remove the Phase 0 staging-anyway clause entirely now that Phase 2 handles exclusion authoritatively.

all_findings.issue: FIND-4: No-dev-report fallback contradicts the universal success criterion. context-20260524-172805.json:36-37 says success_criterion: 'BULK=false stages only task-attributable files. Foreign-session files excluded from staging in normal mode'. The fallback at 115-118 stages everything when no dev-report exists. This is a defensible design (BA's M5 explicitly authorizes graceful degradation), but the success criterion text in context JSON does not carve out the fallback case. Classified as minor because it's a documentation alignment gap, not a runtime defect.
all_findings.recommendation: Either (a) narrow the success_criteria text in future BA specs to 'BULK=false stages only task-attributable files when dev-report exists', or (b) consider fail-closed mode when no dev-report exists. Not blocking this cycle.

failures.issue: Test-writer not dispatched; STANDARD tier requires both artifacts per qa.md Phase 5 item 5
failures._primary_cause_doc: qa_oversight: orchestrator allowed proceed despite missing manifest per Phase 5 item 5 sentinel rule

failures.issue: Phase 0 'staging anyway' contradicts Phase 2 foreign_session_candidate exclusion
failures.recommendation: Update Phase 0 to defer staging decision to Phase 2

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex returned 6 findings: (1) qa-report/completion artifacts missing — IN_SCOPE but addressed by this very report; (2) STANDARD-tier test-writer skip not exempted — IN_SCOPE_REAL_BUG, recorded as critical FIND-1; (3) close.md stdout vs file final-line contradiction — IN_SCOPE_REAL_BUG, recorded as major FIND-2; (4) changelog-analyst.md Phase 0 vs Phase 2 conflict — IN_SCOPE_REAL_BUG, recorded as major FIND-3; (5) no-dev-report fallback contradicts universal claim — IN_SCOPE_MINOR, recorded as minor FIND-4; (6) stale test from prior cycle (20260522-000000) fails under new EVENT_DELTAS — OUT_OF_SCOPE (per-task scoping per qa.md Phase 5), recorded as out_of_scope_observation. Verified codex finding #6 by running pytest — confirmed FAIL with expected 108 got 103.
codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: All 4 in-scope codex findings escalated to qa.all_findings with appropriate severity. Verdict adjusted from draft PASS to FAIL based on FIND-1 (critical) + FIND-2/FIND-3 (major, threaten AC-03/05/06 intent even though AC literal text passes).
